name: BCG vaccine against tuberculosis (Colditz 1994) - fixed/common-effect
citation: Colditz GA, Brewer TF, Berkey CS, et al. JAMA 1994;271(9):698-702. Raw 2x2 as in metafor dat.bcg (Viechtbauer 2010). This fixture exercises the fixed/common-effect inverse-variance path; the FE estimate is dominated by the large trials and differs sharply from the random-effects estimate (heterogeneity I2~92%), which is exactly why it is a useful separate check.
data_provenance: metafor dat.bcg; ai=vaccinated TB+, n1=vaccinated total, ci=control TB+, n2=control total.

reference.source: metafor rma(measure='RR', method='FE'/'EE', data=dat.bcg) - documented common-effect output; engine reproduces logRR=-0.4303, se=0.0405 exactly.

note: Fixed-effect inverse-variance path (closed-form, deterministic) reproduced to the 4th decimal. tau2/I2 omitted (FE assumes tau2=0 by construction). Re-verify against metafor dat.bcg before external citation.
